setup.description: Each eval runs in an isolated temp directory. Before running the skill, the subagent must: (1) create a temp dir via mktemp -d, (2) cd into it, (3) run `taskmd init`, (4) copy fixture tasks from fixtures/tasks/ into the project's tasks/ dir, (5) copy fixtures/src/ into src/. The fixture data provides 5 baseline tasks (001-005) with mixed statuses and priorities, plus source files with TODO comments.

evals.expected_output: Runs taskmd list and displays all 5 baseline tasks with IDs, titles, statuses, and priorities

assertions.text: Output is in a readable table or list format

evals.prompt: show me the details of task 001
evals.expected_output: Runs taskmd get 001, reads the file, presents full details of the SSO bug

assertions.text: Runs taskmd get with the task ID
Reads the task file for full details
Presents ID, title, status, priority, tags, and description

evals.expected_output: Runs taskmd status 002, shows it as pending with medium priority

assertions.text: Runs taskmd status with task ID

evals.prompt: create a new task to implement user notifications via email and in-app, high priority, tags: notifications,backend
evals.expected_output: Runs taskmd add with title and flags, fills in content, validates, confirms creation

assertions.text: Runs taskmd add with title and flags
Replaces placeholder content with real objective, tasks, and acceptance criteria
Reports created file path and task ID

evals.prompt: change task 002 to high priority and add the tag backend
evals.expected_output: Looks up task 002, runs taskmd set with --priority high --add-tag backend, confirms

assertions.text: Looks up the task with taskmd get
Runs taskmd set with --priority high --add-tag backend

evals.expected_output: Checks workflow mode, handles worklog, runs taskmd set 001 --status completed --verify

evals.prompt: what should I work on next?
evals.expected_output: Runs taskmd next, reads recommended task, presents summary. Should recommend task 003 (critical priority) first.

assertions.text: Presents task summary with ID, title, priority, description

evals.prompt: pick up task 003 and start working on it
evals.expected_output: Looks up task 003, marks in-progress, starts worklog, begins working on the XSS fix

assertions.text: Runs taskmd get to find the task
Creates or appends a worklog entry
Starts working on the task (attempts the XSS fix)

evals.prompt: check if all my task files are valid
evals.setup_extra: Create an additional malformed task file tasks/bad-task.md with missing required frontmatter to test error detection.
evals.expected_output: Runs taskmd validate, detects the malformed file, suggests fixes

assertions.text: Suggests how to fix the validation errors

evals.prompt: verify the acceptance criteria for task 003
evals.setup_extra: Add a verify field to task 003's frontmatter: `verify: [{step: 'bash', run: 'grep -r sanitize src/'}]`
evals.expected_output: Runs taskmd verify 003 --format json, interprets results, reports verdict

evals.prompt: task 002 is too big, can you split it into smaller pieces?
evals.expected_output: Looks up task 002 (8 subtasks spanning backend, frontend, tests), assesses as complex enough, creates 3-4 sub-tasks

assertions.text: Reads the task to understand scope
Evaluates complexity and explains why splitting is warranted
Lists created sub-tasks with IDs and titles

evals.prompt: find all the TODO comments in the code and turn them into tasks
evals.expected_output: Runs taskmd todos list, finds 3 TODOs in src/app.go, presents as table, asks which to convert

assertions.text: Displays TODOs in a numbered table format
Asks user which TODOs to convert
